title: Understand a class before changing it
subtitle: VS Code replay showing SAPContext, dependency contracts, source grep, and where-used context.
outcome: A dependency map and safe next-step plan before any code is touched.

start.text: I need to change ZCL_BILLING. First show me what it depends on and where the risky edges are.

context.summary: ARC-1 reads dependency context instead of pulling the full system into the prompt.
context.result: Compressed dependency context for ZCL_BILLING: interface ZIF_BILLING_CALCULATOR, class ZCL_TAX_ENGINE, CDS ZI_BILLING_ITEM, domain ZAMOUNT_DOM. 54 lines returned from 410 lines of related source.

panel.title: Dependency context
panel.body: ZCL_BILLING depends on one public calculator interface, one tax engine class, one CDS view, and a shared amount domain. ARC-1 returns contracts and relevant snippets instead of entire objects.

grep.summary: ARC-1 narrows the source read to the relevant method lines.
grep.result:
METHOD process.
  DATA(total) = calculator->calculate( items ).
  DATA(tax) = tax_engine->compute_tax( total ).
  rv_amount = total + tax.
ENDMETHOD.

panel.title: Relevant source lines
panel.code:
METHOD process.
  DATA(total) = calculator->calculate( items ).
  DATA(tax) = tax_engine->compute_tax( total ).
  rv_amount = total + tax.
ENDMETHOD.

references.summary: ARC-1 asks ADT for references before recommending a change plan.

decision.prompt: What should the replay show next?

options.label: Show change plan
options.description: Summarize the safe edit path from the dependency evidence.

options.label: Inspect tax engine
options.description: Drill into the riskiest dependency before editing billing.

risk-plan.thinking: `SAPContext` gave me the contracts, `SAPRead` showed the actual `process` body, and `SAPNavigate` returned three callers. The real risk isn't `ZCL_BILLING` itself — it's the shared tax engine reaching the nightly `ZCL_REBATE_JOB`, so I'll keep the interface stable and scope the edit to one method.
risk-plan.text:
Here's the safe change plan, built from the dependency context, the `process` source, and the where-used results above.

## Change plan

1. **Keep `ZIF_BILLING_CALCULATOR` stable** — it's the only public contract, so the signature stays untouched and callers don't ripple.
2. **Edit only `ZCL_BILLING->process`** — the tax and amount logic lives in this one method.
3. **Re-run `ZTEST_BILLING_FLOW`** — coverage already exists, so this is your regression gate.
4. **Review `ZCL_REBATE_JOB`** — the nightly batch consumer shares `ZCL_TAX_ENGINE` and its external `TAX_RFC` lookup, so confirm the batch path before release.

This is read-only so far — no objects were changed.

tax-context.summary: The replay drills into the tax engine dependency without losing the original class context.
tax-context.result: ZCL_TAX_ENGINE calls external destination TAX_RFC and table ZTAX_RULES. No public method signature change needed.

panel.title: Tax engine risk
panel.body: The risky part is not the billing class itself. It is the tax engine dependency and the batch caller that reuses the same calculation result.

done.text: That's the ARC-1 pattern in one pass: start with **system context**, then open only the **source and callers** that matter. You get a bounded change plan instead of a full class dump in the prompt.
